steps.title: Find the linear scale factor from the area ratio
steps.content: For similar figures, if $k$ is the linear scale factor, areas scale by $k^2$. The area ratio is $\frac{72}{648} = \frac{1}{9}$. Therefore $k^2 = \frac{1}{9}$, giving $k = \frac{1}{3}$.

steps.title: Apply the scale factor to the longest side
steps.content: The longest side of rectangle ABCD is $36$ inches. Since all linear dimensions scale by $k = \frac{1}{3}$, the longest side of rectangle EFGH is $36 \times \frac{1}{3} = 12$ inches. That matches choice C — C is correct.
